import { defineSchema, defineTable } from "convex/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";

export default defineSchema({
  // ── Auth (existing) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  users: defineTable({
    email: v.string(),
    name: v.string(),
    token: v.string(),
    createdAt: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_token", ["token"])
    .index("by_email", ["email"]),

  // ── Execution state (Convex authoritative) ───────────────────────

  emails: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(), // SQLite id
    source: v.string(),
    direction: v.string(),
    threadId: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    fromEmail: v.string(),
    toEmails: v.string(), // JSON array
    ccEmails: v.optional(v.string()),
    date: v.string(),
    snippet: v.optional(v.string()),
    bodyPreview: v.optional(v.string()),
    labels: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Classification (SQLite authoritative, pushed up)
    class: v.optional(v.string()),
    classConfidence: v.optional(v.float64()),
    classifiedBy: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Unipile IDs
    unipileId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileAccountId: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Sync
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"])
    .index("by_thread", ["userId", "threadId"]),

  mailboxes: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    email: v.string(),
    provider: v.string(),
    transportEmail: v.optional(v.string()),
    rotationGroup: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // active | paused | auth_expired
    warmupStage: v.optional(v.string()),
    warmupStartedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    firstTouchDailyCap: v.float64(),
    followUpDailyCap: v.float64(),
    replyDailyCap: v.float64(),
    minGapMinutes: v.float64(),
    sendWindowStartHour: v.float64(),
    sendWindowEndHour: v.float64(),
    sendWindowTimezone: v.string(),
    reputationScore: v.optional(v.float64()),
    priority: v.optional(v.float64()),
    lastUsedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastBounceAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_email", ["userId", "email"]),

  mailboxSendEvents: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.string(), // externalId of mailbox
    sendType: v.string(), // first_touch | follow_up | reply
    sentAt: v.string(),
    taskId: v.optional(v.string()),
    enrollmentId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_mailbox_day", ["mailboxId", "sentAt"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  sequenceEnrollments: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    sequenceName: v.string(),
    campaignName: v.optional(v.string()),
    mailboxId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    contactName: v.optional(v.string()),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // active | paused | completed | stopped
    statusReason: v.optional(v.string()),
    replyState: v.optional(v.string()),
    currentStep: v.float64(),
    totalSteps: v.float64(),
    lastSentAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastReplyAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    nextDueAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    initialBody: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Unipile
    unipileThreadProviderId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileAccountId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_status", ["userId", "status"])
    .index("by_contact", ["userId", "contactEmail"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  followUpTasks: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    body: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.optional(v.string()),
    stepNumber: v.float64(),
    stepName: v.optional(v.string()),
    sendType: v.string(),
    status: v.string(), // open | completed | canceled
    sendStatus: v.string(), // pending | pending_remote_send | sent | failed | canceled
    dueAt: v.string(),
    completedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    canceledReason: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastAttemptAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastError: v.optional(v.string()),
    attemptCount: v.float64(),
    // Unipile
    unipileMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_due", ["userId", "sendStatus", "dueAt"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  suppressions: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    scope: v.string(), // email | domain
    value: v.string(),
    reason: v.optional(v.string()),
    source: v.string(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_lookup", ["userId", "scope", "value"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  replyEvents: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.string(),
    messageId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    classification: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    snippet: v.optional(v.string()),
    occurredAt: v.string(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_thread", ["userId", "threadId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  sequenceDrafts: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    taskId: v.string(),
    stepNumber: v.float64(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    body: v.optional(v.string()),
    channel: v.string(), // email | linkedin
    status: v.string(), // pending | sent | canceled | stale
    generatedBy: v.optional(v.string()),
    generatedContext: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_task", ["taskId"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  // ── Local state (SQLite authoritative, pushed up for dashboard) ──

  contacts: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    source: v.string(),
    sourceId: v.optional(v.string()),
    email: v.string(),
    name: v.optional(v.string()),
    domain: v.optional(v.string()),
    company: v.optional(v.string()),
    title: v.optional(v.string()),
    phone: v.optional(v.string()),
    leadStatus: v.optional(v.string()),
    owner: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastActivityAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    emailCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    lastEmailedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    class: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_email", ["userId", "email"]),

  learnings: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    type: v.string(),
    content: v.string(),
    sourceSession: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  skillVersions: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    skillName: v.string(),
    version: v.float64(),
    content: v.string(),
    emailCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    learningCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    active: v.optional(v.float64()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  deals: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    source: v.string(),
    sourceId: v.string(),
    name: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.optional(v.string()),
    company: v.optional(v.string()),
    stage: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.optional(v.string()),
    value: v.optional(v.float64()),
    currency: v.optional(v.string()),
    closeDate: v.optional(v.string()),
    owner: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  // ── Context metrics (SQLite authoritative, daily rollups pushed) ──

  contextMetricsDaily: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),     // "2026-03-25:emails list"
    day: v.string(),            // "2026-03-25"
    command: v.string(),        // "emails list"
    calls: v.float64(),
    totalBytes: v.float64(),
    totalTokens: v.float64(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_day", ["userId", "day"])
    .index("by_command", ["userId", "command"]),

  // ── Convex-only tables ───────────────────────────────────────────

  unipileAccounts: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    provider: v.string(), // GOOGLE | LINKEDIN
    unipileAccountId: v.string(),
    email: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // connected | disconnected | auth_expired
    connectedAt: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_provider", ["userId", "provider"])
    .index("by_unipile_id", ["unipileAccountId"]),

  syncState: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    lastSyncVersion: v.float64(),
  }).index("by_user", ["userId"]),

  // Gmail Pub/Sub push notification state
  gmailWatchState: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    email: v.string(),
    historyId: v.string(),
    watchExpiry: v.string(), // ISO8601 -- watch() expires after 7 days
    pendingSync: v.boolean(), // true when Pub/Sub notification received but not yet synced
    lastNotifiedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_email", ["email"]),
});

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Key finding: **Convex already sends emails via `callUnipileSend` in sendApi.ts**. So the approve-to-send path is mostly built. Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Server-Side Reply Handling + Slack Approve-to-Send

## Problem

When a prospect replies to an outbound email at 8pm, the response sits in a queue until the user opens their laptop the next morning. Speed kills in outbound: a 5-minute response converts 21x better than a 30-minute response. The local dispatcher (launchd) only runs when the laptop is on, creating a 10-14 hour dead zone every night.

## Solution

Move reply detection from the local machine to the server (Convex). When a reply arrives, Convex fetches it via Gmail API (using the user's stored refresh token), classifies it, and triggers a RemoteTrigger to draft an intelligent response. The user gets a Slack notification with the draft and can approve it from their phone. The approved reply is sent via the existing Unipile send path in Convex. No laptop needed.

## Architecture

```
Gmail Pub/Sub notification (24/7)
  -> Convex webhook (already built)
  -> Convex fetches reply via Gmail API (stored refresh token) [NEW]
  -> Convex classifies reply (keyword heuristics) [NEW]
  -> Convex stores in replyDrafts table [NEW]
  -> RemoteTrigger drafts response (hourly, claude.ai cloud) [NEW]
  -> Convex sends Slack notification with approve/edit/skip buttons [NEW]
  -> User taps Approve in Slack
  -> Slack callback -> Convex HTTP endpoint [NEW]
  -> Convex sends reply via callUnipileSend (EXISTING)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Gmail API for reading, Unipile for sending.** The refresh token is stored in Convex for reading replies only. Sending goes through the existing `callUnipileSend` path which handles threading, caps, and suppression.

2. **Classification in Convex, drafting in RemoteTrigger.** Keyword-based classification (interested/not_interested/bounced/etc.) runs immediately in Convex. Intelligent response drafting runs in RemoteTrigger (hourly) because it needs Claude and repo context (voice profile, skills).

3. **Slack as the approval channel.** Not email (would be recursive), not SMS (expensive). Slack interactive messages give approve/edit/skip buttons. Edit opens a modal with the draft text.

4. **Local dispatcher is fallback, not primary.** Server-side detection is the primary path. Local dispatcher still runs for faster detection when laptop is open and for operations that need local DB.

5. **Encrypted token storage.** Gmail refresh tokens are encrypted at rest in Convex using a per-user encryption key derived from the user's auth token.

## New Convex Tables

### gmailCredentials
Stores Gmail OAuth refresh tokens for server-side API access.
```
userId: string
email: string
encryptedRefreshToken: string  // AES-256-GCM encrypted
tokenIv: string               // initialization vector
scopes: string                // comma-separated scopes granted
storedAt: string              // ISO8601
lastUsedAt: string            // ISO8601, updated on each use
status: string                // active | revoked | expired
```

### replyDrafts
Drafted responses to prospect replies, pending user approval.
```
userId: string
replyEventId: string          // links to replyEvents.externalId
contactEmail: string
contactName: string
threadId: string
originalSubject: string
originalSnippet: string
classification: string        // interested, objection, referral, etc.
draftSubject: string
draftBody: string
draftedBy: string             // "remote_trigger" | "template" | "user"
confidence: string            // high | medium | low
status: string                // pending_draft | pending_review | approved | sent | rejected | expired
slackMessageTs: string        // Slack message timestamp for updating
notifiedAt: string
reviewedAt: string
sentAt: string
expiresAt: string             // drafts expire after 24h
```

### slackConnections
Slack workspace connections for notifications.
```
userId: string
teamId: string                // Slack workspace ID
channelId: string             // notification channel
botToken: string              // encrypted Slack bot token
botTokenIv: string
installedAt: string
status: string                // active | revoked
```

## New Convex HTTP Endpoints

| Path | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/slack-callback` | POST | Slack interactive message callback (approve/edit/skip) |
| `/api/connect-slack` | POST | Initiate Slack OAuth |
| `/api/connect-slack-success` | GET | Slack OAuth callback |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | GET | List pending drafts (for CLI/RemoteTrigger) |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | POST | Create/update draft (from RemoteTrigger) |

## New Convex Actions

| Function | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `fetchGmailReply` | `markPendingSync` mutation (on Pub/Sub notification) | Use stored refresh token to call Gmail history.list, fetch new messages |
| `classifyAndRecordReply` | `fetchGmailReply` action | Run keyword classification, create replyDrafts row with status=pending_draft |
| `sendSlackNotification` | After draft is created (status changes to pending_review) | Send Slack interactive message |
| `handleSlackApproval` | Slack callback | Call existing `callUnipileSend`, update draft status |

## RemoteTrigger Specification

- **Schedule:** Every hour (`0 * * * *` UTC)
- **Environment:** Anthropic cloud (env_015asbMBaj4NsdeUxymCXAeR)
- **Model:** claude-sonnet-4-6
- **Prompt:** Fetch pending_draft items from Convex, read voice profile + skills from repo, draft responses, POST drafts back to Convex
- **Tools:** Bash, Read, Glob, Grep (to read repo files for voice/persona context)

## Slack Interactive Message Format

```json
{
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "text": "Reply from Sarah Chen"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "\"Love to learn more about how you handle the migration piece\""
    },
    {
      "type": "divider"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "Draft reply (87 words):\n\nSarah, great to hear..."
    },
    {
      "type": "actions",
      "elements": [
        { "type": "button", "text": "Send", "style": "primary", "action_id": "approve_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Edit", "action_id": "edit_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Skip", "action_id": "skip_reply" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Security

1. **Gmail refresh tokens** are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. Encryption key is derived from a server-side secret + per-user salt. Never logged or returned in API responses.
2. **Slack bot tokens** are similarly encrypted.
3. **Slack callback verification** uses Slack's signing secret to verify webhook authenticity.
4. **Draft expiry:** Drafts expire after 24 hours to prevent stale responses being sent.

## Edge Cases

1. **Multiple replies in quick succession:** Pub/Sub may batch notifications. `fetchGmailReply` uses `history.list` with the last known historyId, so it catches all messages since the last check, not just the notification trigger.
2. **Token refresh failure:** If the stored refresh token is invalid (user revoked access), mark `gmailCredentials.status = 'expired'` and notify via Slack: "Gmail access expired. Run `nurge connect gmail` to reconnect."
3. **RemoteTrigger down:** If the hourly trigger fails, drafts sit in `pending_draft` state. Next trigger run picks them up. No data loss.
4. **Slack not connected:** If no Slack connection, drafts wait for the next CLI session. The SessionStart hook shows pending drafts.
5. **User edits in Slack:** The Edit button opens a Slack modal with the draft text. User modifies and submits. Convex updates the draft body and sends.
6. **Duplicate sends:** The `callUnipileSend` path already handles deduplication via task status checks. Draft status transitions are atomic (pending_review -> approved -> sent).

## Out of Scope

- Signal monitoring (separate feature, session-only for now)
- Enrichment via RemoteTrigger
- Multi-channel replies (LinkedIn messages)
- Team/multi-user Slack notifications

toolUseResult.content:
# Server-Side Reply Handling + Slack Approve-to-Send

## Problem

When a prospect replies to an outbound email at 8pm, the response sits in a queue until the user opens their laptop the next morning. Speed kills in outbound: a 5-minute response converts 21x better than a 30-minute response. The local dispatcher (launchd) only runs when the laptop is on, creating a 10-14 hour dead zone every night.

## Solution

Move reply detection from the local machine to the server (Convex). When a reply arrives, Convex fetches it via Gmail API (using the user's stored refresh token), classifies it, and triggers a RemoteTrigger to draft an intelligent response. The user gets a Slack notification with the draft and can approve it from their phone. The approved reply is sent via the existing Unipile send path in Convex. No laptop needed.

## Architecture

```
Gmail Pub/Sub notification (24/7)
  -> Convex webhook (already built)
  -> Convex fetches reply via Gmail API (stored refresh token) [NEW]
  -> Convex classifies reply (keyword heuristics) [NEW]
  -> Convex stores in replyDrafts table [NEW]
  -> RemoteTrigger drafts response (hourly, claude.ai cloud) [NEW]
  -> Convex sends Slack notification with approve/edit/skip buttons [NEW]
  -> User taps Approve in Slack
  -> Slack callback -> Convex HTTP endpoint [NEW]
  -> Convex sends reply via callUnipileSend (EXISTING)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Gmail API for reading, Unipile for sending.** The refresh token is stored in Convex for reading replies only. Sending goes through the existing `callUnipileSend` path which handles threading, caps, and suppression.

2. **Classification in Convex, drafting in RemoteTrigger.** Keyword-based classification (interested/not_interested/bounced/etc.) runs immediately in Convex. Intelligent response drafting runs in RemoteTrigger (hourly) because it needs Claude and repo context (voice profile, skills).

3. **Slack as the approval channel.** Not email (would be recursive), not SMS (expensive). Slack interactive messages give approve/edit/skip buttons. Edit opens a modal with the draft text.

4. **Local dispatcher is fallback, not primary.** Server-side detection is the primary path. Local dispatcher still runs for faster detection when laptop is open and for operations that need local DB.

5. **Encrypted token storage.** Gmail refresh tokens are encrypted at rest in Convex using a per-user encryption key derived from the user's auth token.

## New Convex Tables

### gmailCredentials
Stores Gmail OAuth refresh tokens for server-side API access.
```
userId: string
email: string
encryptedRefreshToken: string  // AES-256-GCM encrypted
tokenIv: string               // initialization vector
scopes: string                // comma-separated scopes granted
storedAt: string              // ISO8601
lastUsedAt: string            // ISO8601, updated on each use
status: string                // active | revoked | expired
```

### replyDrafts
Drafted responses to prospect replies, pending user approval.
```
userId: string
replyEventId: string          // links to replyEvents.externalId
contactEmail: string
contactName: string
threadId: string
originalSubject: string
originalSnippet: string
classification: string        // interested, objection, referral, etc.
draftSubject: string
draftBody: string
draftedBy: string             // "remote_trigger" | "template" | "user"
confidence: string            // high | medium | low
status: string                // pending_draft | pending_review | approved | sent | rejected | expired
slackMessageTs: string        // Slack message timestamp for updating
notifiedAt: string
reviewedAt: string
sentAt: string
expiresAt: string             // drafts expire after 24h
```

### slackConnections
Slack workspace connections for notifications.
```
userId: string
teamId: string                // Slack workspace ID
channelId: string             // notification channel
botToken: string              // encrypted Slack bot token
botTokenIv: string
installedAt: string
status: string                // active | revoked
```

## New Convex HTTP Endpoints

| Path | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/slack-callback` | POST | Slack interactive message callback (approve/edit/skip) |
| `/api/connect-slack` | POST | Initiate Slack OAuth |
| `/api/connect-slack-success` | GET | Slack OAuth callback |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | GET | List pending drafts (for CLI/RemoteTrigger) |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | POST | Create/update draft (from RemoteTrigger) |

## New Convex Actions

| Function | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `fetchGmailReply` | `markPendingSync` mutation (on Pub/Sub notification) | Use stored refresh token to call Gmail history.list, fetch new messages |
| `classifyAndRecordReply` | `fetchGmailReply` action | Run keyword classification, create replyDrafts row with status=pending_draft |
| `sendSlackNotification` | After draft is created (status changes to pending_review) | Send Slack interactive message |
| `handleSlackApproval` | Slack callback | Call existing `callUnipileSend`, update draft status |

## RemoteTrigger Specification

- **Schedule:** Every hour (`0 * * * *` UTC)
- **Environment:** Anthropic cloud (env_015asbMBaj4NsdeUxymCXAeR)
- **Model:** claude-sonnet-4-6
- **Prompt:** Fetch pending_draft items from Convex, read voice profile + skills from repo, draft responses, POST drafts back to Convex
- **Tools:** Bash, Read, Glob, Grep (to read repo files for voice/persona context)

## Slack Interactive Message Format

```json
{
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "text": "Reply from Sarah Chen"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "\"Love to learn more about how you handle the migration piece\""
    },
    {
      "type": "divider"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "Draft reply (87 words):\n\nSarah, great to hear..."
    },
    {
      "type": "actions",
      "elements": [
        { "type": "button", "text": "Send", "style": "primary", "action_id": "approve_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Edit", "action_id": "edit_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Skip", "action_id": "skip_reply" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Security

1. **Gmail refresh tokens** are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. Encryption key is derived from a server-side secret + per-user salt. Never logged or returned in API responses.
2. **Slack bot tokens** are similarly encrypted.
3. **Slack callback verification** uses Slack's signing secret to verify webhook authenticity.
4. **Draft expiry:** Drafts expire after 24 hours to prevent stale responses being sent.

## Edge Cases

1. **Multiple replies in quick succession:** Pub/Sub may batch notifications. `fetchGmailReply` uses `history.list` with the last known historyId, so it catches all messages since the last check, not just the notification trigger.
2. **Token refresh failure:** If the stored refresh token is invalid (user revoked access), mark `gmailCredentials.status = 'expired'` and notify via Slack: "Gmail access expired. Run `nurge connect gmail` to reconnect."
3. **RemoteTrigger down:** If the hourly trigger fails, drafts sit in `pending_draft` state. Next trigger run picks them up. No data loss.
4. **Slack not connected:** If no Slack connection, drafts wait for the next CLI session. The SessionStart hook shows pending drafts.
5. **User edits in Slack:** The Edit button opens a Slack modal with the draft text. User modifies and submits. Convex updates the draft body and sends.
6. **Duplicate sends:** The `callUnipileSend` path already handles deduplication via task status checks. Draft status transitions are atomic (pending_review -> approved -> sent).

## Out of Scope

- Signal monitoring (separate feature, session-only for now)
- Enrichment via RemoteTrigger
- Multi-channel replies (LinkedIn messages)
- Team/multi-user Slack notifications

---

Now to your next question: **calendar link in the flow + closing the conversation.**

You're thinking about the full lifecycle:

```
Reply detected → Draft sent → Prospect says "yes let's meet"
  → Send calendar link (automated?)
  → Meeting booked
  → Conversation marked CLOSED (won/lost/stale)
```

Two questions to clarify:

1. **Calendar link** - are you using Calendly/Cal.com/Google Calendar? Or do you want Nurge to propose times directly from the user's Google Calendar (since Gmail is already connected)?

2. **Closed conversation** - what signals should trigger close?
   - **Won:** Meeting booked (calendar event confirmed)
   - **Lost:** "not interested" / unsubscribe / no reply after full sequence
   - **Stale:** No activity for X days after initial reply
   
   Or is there more to it (like CRM deal stage sync)?

message.content: what would make this a CRM in the future?

content.text:
Look at what you already have:

| CRM Function | Nurge Today | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| **Contacts** | `contacts` table with enrichment, tier, scoring | No activity timeline view |
| **Pipeline stages** | `sequence_enrollments` (active/paused/stopped/completed) | These are *sequence* stages, not *deal* stages |
| **Activity tracking** | `emails`, `send_attempts`, `reply_events`, `engagement_events` | All tracked, not unified per-contact |
| **Deals** | `deals` table exists in Convex schema | Empty structure, no lifecycle |
| **Revenue** | Deal has `value`, `currency`, `closeDate` fields | Never populated |
| **Reporting** | `nurge-analytics`, `nurge-review` skills | Sequence metrics only, no revenue |

You're closer than you think. The data exists. The gap is **lifecycle after the reply.**

## What's Missing: The Post-Reply Lifecycle

Today Nurge's world ends here:

```
Source → Research → Qualify → Contact → Follow-up → Reply → ???
```

A CRM closes the loop:

```
Source → Research → Qualify → Contact → Follow-up → Reply
  → Meeting Booked → Proposal → Negotiation → Won/Lost
  → Revenue attributed back to source
```

## The Five Things That Make It a CRM

**1. Deal stages (not sequence stages)**

Right now `enrollment.status` tracks outreach state. You need a separate `deal.stage` that tracks buyer state:

```
lead → qualified → meeting_booked → proposal → negotiation → won | lost | stale
```

The transition from `enrollment` to `deal` happens when someone replies interested. That's exactly what your Slack flow does - "interested" reply → draft → approved → meeting → deal.

**2. Unified contact timeline**

One command that shows everything Nurge knows about a person:

```
nurge contact show sarah@datastack.com

Sarah Chen | CTO @ DataStack | Tier 1
───────────────────────────────────
Mar 15  Sourced from LinkedIn import
Mar 16  Enriched (LinkedIn, GitHub, website)
Mar 16  Researched - Series B, hiring 3 engineers
Mar 17  Email 1 sent: "Saw your Series B..."
Mar 20  Email 2 sent (follow-up)
Mar 22  REPLIED: "Love to learn more"
Mar 22  Draft approved via Slack, reply sent
Mar 23  Meeting booked: Thu Mar 27 2pm
Mar 27  Meeting held
Mar 28  Proposal sent
Apr 03  DEAL WON: $24,000 ARR
───────────────────────────────────
Source: LinkedIn | Sequence: series-b-cto | Days to close: 19
```

All the data for this already exists across your tables. You just need a unified query.

**3. Calendar integration (meeting = stage transition)**

When a prospect replies "yes let's chat" and the user sends a calendar link:
- Detect calendar event creation (Google Calendar API - same OAuth token you're already storing in Convex)
- Auto-transition deal stage: `qualified → meeting_booked`
- After meeting date passes: auto-transition to `proposal` (or prompt user)

**4. Revenue attribution**

When a deal closes, trace it back:
- Which source? (LinkedIn import, Gmail history, manual)
- Which sequence? (series-b-cto, cold-outreach-q1)
- Which persona? (CTO, VP Eng)
- How many touches? (3 emails, 1 LinkedIn, 1 meeting)
- Days to close? (19 days)

This tells the user: "CTOs from LinkedIn close 3x faster than VPs from email lists." That's the insight that makes Nurge irreplaceable.

**5. Close the loop: learning from outcomes**

Today Nurge learns from replies (tone, preferences). A CRM-Nurge learns from **closes**:

- "Emails mentioning Series B funding get 12% reply rate AND 8% close rate"
- "CTO persona: 15% reply, 3.2% close. VP Eng: 8% reply, 5.1% close"
- "3-touch sequences close at 2x the rate of 5-touch sequences"

Feed this back into skills. `nurge-outbound` doesn't just optimize for replies - it optimizes for revenue.

## What You DON'T Need

- Contact forms / inbound lead capture (not your market)
- Customer support ticketing
- Complex pipeline customization (fixed stages work for outbound)
- Multi-user permissions/roles (single person for now)
- Forecasting models (too early, need data first)

## The Path

| Phase | What | When |
|---|---|---|
| **Now** | Reply handling + Slack approve-to-send (just planned) | This sprint |
| **Next** | Calendar link in reply flow + meeting detection | Next sprint |
| **Then** | Deal stages (auto-transition on meeting/close) | After calendar |
| **Then** | Contact timeline command | After deal stages |
| **Then** | Revenue attribution + outcome learning | After first deals close |

Each phase builds on the last. You're not building a CRM from scratch - you're closing the lifecycle loop that's already 60% built.

Should I add the calendar + deal stage design to the spec, or do you want to ship the reply handling first and iterate?

message.content: in multi stakeholder cases, gmail stops there and AE takes charge

content.text:
That's the boundary. Nurge's world:

```
Source → Research → Contact → Reply → Meeting Booked → HANDOFF
                                                         ↓
                                            AE takes over (HubSpot/Salesforce)
```

So Nurge is not a full CRM. It's an **outbound pipeline CRM** that owns everything up to the handoff, then tracks the outcome for learning.

Two modes:

| Mode | Who closes | Nurge's role |
|---|---|---|
| **Founder-led** (single stakeholder) | Same person | Full loop: outbound → close |
| **Team** (multi-stakeholder) | AE | Outbound → meeting booked → handoff → wait for outcome |

For the handoff case, Nurge needs:

1. **Push to CRM** at handoff point - contact, enrichment, conversation history, signals, research dossier all go to HubSpot/Salesforce. The AE gets everything, not just a name.

2. **Track the handoff** - `deal.stage = 'handed_off'`, `deal.owner = 'AE name'`, `deal.crm_id = 'hubspot:deal:123'`

3. **Get the outcome back** - CRM webhook or periodic sync: did it close? How much? When? This is the only data that flows back from the AE's world.

4. **Learn from outcomes** - "I handed off 30 meetings, 8 closed, here's what the closed ones had in common." That feedback loop makes Nurge's sourcing and messaging better even though Nurge didn't close the deal.

So the calendar link flow becomes:

```
Reply: "Let's chat"
  → Slack: [Send calendar link] [Send reply] [Skip]
  → User sends calendar link
  → Meeting detected (Google Calendar)
  → If CRM connected: push to CRM, mark handed_off
  → If no CRM (founder-led): stay in Nurge, track through close
```

This keeps Nurge focused. It doesn't try to be Salesforce. It owns the top of funnel and learns from the bottom.

Should I add this handoff boundary to the spec before we start building?

message.content: but Nurge need closed los/won data to build context?

content.text:
Yes. That's the whole point. Without outcome data, Nurge is optimizing blind - it can tell you what gets replies, but not what gets revenue.

The question is: **how does the won/lost data get back to Nurge?**

Three paths depending on the user's setup:

| Setup | How outcome returns | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| **Founder-led (no CRM)** | User tells Nurge: "we won DataStack" or "lost Acme" | Zero - just a command |
| **CRM connected** | HubSpot/Salesforce webhook fires on deal stage change | Automatic |
| **AE handoff, no CRM webhook** | Nurge asks periodically: "You handed off 5 deals last month. Any close?" | Low-touch prompt |

**Path 1 is the one that matters now.** Your users are founders, bootstrapped, probably no CRM. So:

```
nurge deal won sarah@datastack.com --value 24000 --currency usd
nurge deal lost mike@acme.com --reason "went with competitor"
```

Or even simpler - Nurge asks during the session:

```
"You booked a meeting with Sarah Chen 2 weeks ago. Did that close?"
  → Won ($24K)
  → Lost (reason?)
  → Still in progress
  → Handed off
```

**What Nurge does with that data:**

Every won/lost feeds back into the knowledge atoms:

```
Won: Sarah Chen, CTO, DataStack (Series B, 200 employees)
  → Source: LinkedIn import
  → Sequence: series-b-cto (3-touch)
  → Email 1 hook: referenced their Series B
  → Reply after: 5 days, email 2
  → Days to close: 19
  → Value: $24K

Lost: Mike Ross, VP Eng, Acme (Enterprise, 2000 employees)
  → Source: Gmail history
  → Sequence: enterprise-vp (5-touch)
  → Reply after: 3 days, email 1
  → Reason: competitor
  → Days to lose: 45
```

This becomes:

- **Persona scoring:** CTO closes at 3.2%, VP Eng at 1.1%. Weight outreach toward CTOs.
- **Sequence optimization:** 3-touch closes better than 5-touch for Series B companies.
- **Hook analysis:** "Series B funding" hook → 12% reply → 8% close. "Hiring signal" hook → 15% reply → 2% close (replies but doesn't buy).
- **ICP refinement:** 200-employee companies close, 2000-employee companies don't. Tighten ICP.
- **Anti-patterns:** "competitor" is the #1 loss reason. Add competitor detection to qualification.

**The pipeline skill learns the difference between "gets replies" and "makes money."** That's what makes Nurge smarter than every other outbound tool.

This is simple to build. The `deals` table already exists in your Convex schema with `value`, `stage`, `closeDate`. You just need:

1. `nurge deal` CLI commands (won/lost/update)
2. A session hook that checks for stale meetings ("you met Sarah 14 days ago, what happened?")
3. Feed outcomes into knowledge atoms via the existing learning/distill pipeline

Want me to add this to the spec as a follow-up phase, or fold it into the current plan?

message.content: we can read from gmail or CRM, data collection hould be paive

content.text:
Right. No asking the user. Detect it.

**From Gmail (already connected):**
- "Thank you for your purchase" / "Welcome aboard" / "Contract signed" emails in the thread → won
- "We've decided to go with..." / "Not moving forward" → lost
- Calendar invite accepted → meeting confirmed
- Calendar invite declined/canceled → meeting fell through
- Proposal/invoice attachments sent → deal progressing
- Thread goes silent for 30+ days after meeting → stale

**From CRM (if connected):**
- HubSpot deal stage changes to "Closed Won" / "Closed Lost" → direct signal
- Deal value, close date, loss reason all come from the webhook

**From Google Calendar (same OAuth token):**
- Meeting happened (event in the past, not canceled) → deal progressing
- No follow-up email within 7 days of meeting → stale signal

The classification logic is the same pattern as `classifyReply()` - keyword heuristics on email content. The thread already exists in Nurge. You just need to keep reading it past the reply stage.

```
Thread: sarah@datastack.com
  Email 1: Outbound (nurge sent)
  Email 2: Follow-up (nurge sent)
  Email 3: Reply "let's chat" → INTERESTED (already detected)
  Email 4: Calendar link sent
  Email 5: "Great meeting, here's the proposal" → PROGRESSING
  Email 6: "We'd like to move forward" → WON (passive detection)
```

The local dispatcher already syncs the full thread via `history.list`. It just stops caring after the reply. Extend it to keep scanning the thread for outcome signals.

No new data collection. No user input. Just keep reading what's already flowing through Gmail.

Should I add this as a phase in the plan - "passive outcome detection from existing email threads"?

message.content: only track CRM for this now

content.text:
Smart. CRM is the source of truth for deal outcomes. Gmail thread scanning is fuzzy and error-prone. CRM has explicit won/lost with values and dates.

So the flow:

```
Nurge sources + contacts + books meeting
  → Pushes contact + deal to HubSpot/Salesforce at handoff
  → CRM webhook fires on deal stage change
  → Convex receives: won/lost, value, reason, close date
  → Feeds into knowledge atoms
  → Nurge gets smarter
```

HubSpot connector already exists in your codebase (`internal/connector/definitions/hubspot.yaml`). The missing piece is the **webhook listener** for deal stage changes.

I'll add this as Task 12 in the plan. One table (`dealOutcomes`), one webhook endpoint, one learning distill path. No Gmail scanning, no user prompts.

Want me to update the plan now, or ship the reply handling first?

message.content: yes

content.text: Which one - update the plan now, or ship reply handling first?

message.content: also GMAIL in terms no CRM

content.text:
Got it. Both paths:

- **CRM connected:** Webhook is source of truth for won/lost
- **No CRM:** Passively detect from Gmail threads (best effort)

**Files:**
- No code files. Uses the RemoteTrigger tool to create a scheduled agent.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the RemoteTrigger prompt**

The prompt the remote agent will run with. Save this as a reference document:

Create: `docs/remote-trigger-reply-drafter.md`

```markdown
# Reply Drafter - Remote Trigger Prompt

You are Nurge's background reply drafter. You run hourly on Anthropic's cloud.

## What you do

1. Fetch pending reply drafts from Convex:
   ```bash
   curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $NURGE_TOKEN" \
     "$CONVEX_URL/api/reply-drafts"
   ```

2. For each draft with status "pending_draft":
   a. Read the voice profile from the repo:
      - .claude/skills/nurge-replies/SKILL.md (reply handling guidelines)
      - .claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md (product context)
      - .nurge/config.json (voice, personas, positioning)

   b. Based on the classification, draft an appropriate response:
      - INTERESTED: Warm, suggest specific meeting times, 50-90 words
      - REPLIED (generic): Acknowledge, ask clarifying question, 50-90 words
      - REFERRAL: Thank them, ask for warm intro, 50-90 words
      - OUT_OF_OFFICE: Note return date, schedule follow-up after

   c. Match the user's voice profile (formality, opener style, CTA style)

   d. POST the draft back to Convex:
      ```bash
      curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $NURGE_TOKEN" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        "$CONVEX_URL/api/reply-drafts" \
        -d '{"draftId":"...","draftSubject":"...","draftBody":"...","confidence":"high"}'
      ```

3. If no pending drafts, exit quietly.

## Rules
- Emails MUST be 50-90 words max
- No filler, no preambles
- Match the user's tone from their voice profile
- Never fabricate social proof
- Include word count check before submitting
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create the RemoteTrigger via API**

Use the RemoteTrigger tool with action "create":

```json
{
  "name": "nurge-reply-drafter",
  "cron_expression": "7 * * * *",
  "enabled": true,
  "job_config": {
    "ccr": {
      "environment_id": "env_015asbMBaj4NsdeUxymCXAeR",
      "session_context": {
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
        "sources": [
          {"git_repository": {"url": "https://github.com/usenurge/nurgesh"}}
        ],
        "allowed_tools": ["Bash", "Read", "Glob", "Grep"]
      },
      "events": [
        {"data": {
          "uuid": "<generate-fresh-uuid>",
          "session_id": "",
          "type": "user",
          "parent_tool_use_id": null,
          "message": {
            "content": "<full prompt from step 1>",
            "role": "user"
          }
        }}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify trigger was created**

Use RemoteTrigger with action "list" to confirm the trigger exists.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the reference doc**

```bash
git add docs/remote-trigger-reply-drafter.md
git commit -m "docs: RemoteTrigger prompt for reply drafting agent"

   156→1. **Multiple replies in quick succession:** Pub/Sub may batch notifications. `fetchGmailReply` uses `history.list` with the last known historyId, so it catches all messages since the last check, not just the notification trigger.
   157→2. **Token refresh failure:** If the stored refresh token is invalid (user revoked access), mark `gmailCredentials.status = 'expired'` and notify via Slack: "Gmail access expired. Run `nurge connect gmail` to reconnect."
   158→3. **RemoteTrigger down:** If the hourly trigger fails, drafts sit in `pending_draft` state. Next trigger run picks them up. No data loss.
   159→4. **Slack not connected:** If no Slack connection, drafts wait for the next CLI session. The SessionStart hook shows pending drafts.
   160→5. **User edits in Slack:** The Edit button opens a Slack modal with the draft text. User modifies and submits. Convex updates the draft body and sends.
   161→6. **Duplicate sends:** The `callUnipileSend` path already handles deduplication via task status checks. Draft status transitions are atomic (pending_review -> approved -> sent).

file.content:
1. **Multiple replies in quick succession:** Pub/Sub may batch notifications. `fetchGmailReply` uses `history.list` with the last known historyId, so it catches all messages since the last check, not just the notification trigger.
2. **Token refresh failure:** If the stored refresh token is invalid (user revoked access), mark `gmailCredentials.status = 'expired'` and notify via Slack: "Gmail access expired. Run `nurge connect gmail` to reconnect."
3. **RemoteTrigger down:** If the hourly trigger fails, drafts sit in `pending_draft` state. Next trigger run picks them up. No data loss.
4. **Slack not connected:** If no Slack connection, drafts wait for the next CLI session. The SessionStart hook shows pending drafts.
5. **User edits in Slack:** The Edit button opens a Slack modal with the draft text. User modifies and submits. Convex updates the draft body and sends.
6. **Duplicate sends:** The `callUnipileSend` path already handles deduplication via task status checks. Draft status transitions are atomic (pending_review -> approved -> sent).

## Out of Scope

- Signal monitoring (separate feature, session-only for now)
- Enrichment via RemoteTrigger
- Multi-channel replies (LinkedIn messages)
- Team/multi-user Slack notifications

input.new_string:
## Deal Outcome Tracking (Closing the Loop)

Nurge must learn what gets revenue, not just replies. Two paths for getting won/lost data back:

### Path 1: CRM Webhook (Primary, when CRM connected)

HubSpot webhook fires on deal stage change. Convex receives the outcome.
- Won: deal value, close date, contact email
- Lost: loss reason, contact email
- Nurge matches to the enrollment that sourced it, computes attribution

### Path 2: Gmail Thread Scanning (Fallback, no CRM)

Passively detect outcomes from existing tracked threads:
- Won signals: "contract signed", "welcome aboard", "invoice attached", "let's get started"
- Lost signals: "decided to go with", "going in a different direction", "not moving forward"
- Stale: thread with reply but no activity for 30+ days

Gmail detection is best-effort, medium confidence. CRM is source of truth when available.

### Path 3: Ask the User (Last Resort)

Session hook checks for stale meetings: "You met Sarah Chen 14 days ago. Any update?"
- Won / Lost / Still in progress / Handed off

### Attribution Data (stored per outcome)

- Source: how Nurge found this lead (LinkedIn import, Gmail history, manual)
- Sequence: which outreach sequence was used
- Touch count: total emails sent before close
- Days to close: from first email to outcome
- Persona: which buyer persona matched

### What Outcomes Feed

- **Persona scoring:** Which personas close vs which just reply
- **Sequence optimization:** Which cadences produce revenue
- **Hook analysis:** Which email hooks lead to closed deals
- **ICP refinement:** Company size/industry patterns in wins vs losses
- **Loss pattern detection:** Common loss reasons to address in messaging

### New Convex Table: dealOutcomes

```
userId, source, sourceId, contactEmail, company, enrollmentId,
outcome (won|lost|stale), value, currency, lossReason, closeDate,
detectedAt, sourcedBy, sequenceName, touchCount, daysToClose, personaMatch
```

## Out of Scope

- Signal monitoring (separate feature, session-only for now)
- Enrichment via RemoteTrigger
- Multi-channel replies (LinkedIn messages)
- Team/multi-user Slack notifications
- Full CRM pipeline management (Nurge owns top of funnel only)
- Post-close customer tracking (renewals, upsell)

# Server-Side Reply Handling + Slack Approve-to-Send

## Problem

When a prospect replies to an outbound email at 8pm, the response sits in a queue until the user opens their laptop the next morning. Speed kills in outbound: a 5-minute response converts 21x better than a 30-minute response. The local dispatcher (launchd) only runs when the laptop is on, creating a 10-14 hour dead zone every night.

## Solution

Move reply detection from the local machine to the server (Convex). When a reply arrives, Convex fetches it via Gmail API (using the user's stored refresh token), classifies it, and triggers a RemoteTrigger to draft an intelligent response. The user gets a Slack notification with the draft and can approve it from their phone. The approved reply is sent via the existing Unipile send path in Convex. No laptop needed.

## Architecture

```
Gmail Pub/Sub notification (24/7)
  -> Convex webhook (already built)
  -> Convex fetches reply via Gmail API (stored refresh token) [NEW]
  -> Convex classifies reply (keyword heuristics) [NEW]
  -> Convex stores in replyDrafts table [NEW]
  -> RemoteTrigger drafts response (hourly, claude.ai cloud) [NEW]
  -> Convex sends Slack notification with approve/edit/skip buttons [NEW]
  -> User taps Approve in Slack
  -> Slack callback -> Convex HTTP endpoint [NEW]
  -> Convex sends reply via callUnipileSend (EXISTING)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Gmail API for reading, Unipile for sending.** The refresh token is stored in Convex for reading replies only. Sending goes through the existing `callUnipileSend` path which handles threading, caps, and suppression.

2. **Classification in Convex, drafting in RemoteTrigger.** Keyword-based classification (interested/not_interested/bounced/etc.) runs immediately in Convex. Intelligent response drafting runs in RemoteTrigger (hourly) because it needs Claude and repo context (voice profile, skills).

3. **Slack as the approval channel.** Not email (would be recursive), not SMS (expensive). Slack interactive messages give approve/edit/skip buttons. Edit opens a modal with the draft text.

4. **Local dispatcher is fallback, not primary.** Server-side detection is the primary path. Local dispatcher still runs for faster detection when laptop is open and for operations that need local DB.

5. **Encrypted token storage.** Gmail refresh tokens are encrypted at rest in Convex using a per-user encryption key derived from the user's auth token.

## New Convex Tables

### gmailCredentials
Stores Gmail OAuth refresh tokens for server-side API access.
```
userId: string
email: string
encryptedRefreshToken: string  // AES-256-GCM encrypted
tokenIv: string               // initialization vector
scopes: string                // comma-separated scopes granted
storedAt: string              // ISO8601
lastUsedAt: string            // ISO8601, updated on each use
status: string                // active | revoked | expired
```

### replyDrafts
Drafted responses to prospect replies, pending user approval.
```
userId: string
replyEventId: string          // links to replyEvents.externalId
contactEmail: string
contactName: string
threadId: string
originalSubject: string
originalSnippet: string
classification: string        // interested, objection, referral, etc.
draftSubject: string
draftBody: string
draftedBy: string             // "remote_trigger" | "template" | "user"
confidence: string            // high | medium | low
status: string                // pending_draft | pending_review | approved | sent | rejected | expired
slackMessageTs: string        // Slack message timestamp for updating
notifiedAt: string
reviewedAt: string
sentAt: string
expiresAt: string             // drafts expire after 24h
```

### slackConnections
Slack workspace connections for notifications.
```
userId: string
teamId: string                // Slack workspace ID
channelId: string             // notification channel
botToken: string              // encrypted Slack bot token
botTokenIv: string
installedAt: string
status: string                // active | revoked
```

## New Convex HTTP Endpoints

| Path | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/slack-callback` | POST | Slack interactive message callback (approve/edit/skip) |
| `/api/connect-slack` | POST | Initiate Slack OAuth |
| `/api/connect-slack-success` | GET | Slack OAuth callback |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | GET | List pending drafts (for CLI/RemoteTrigger) |
| `/api/reply-drafts` | POST | Create/update draft (from RemoteTrigger) |

## New Convex Actions

| Function | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `fetchGmailReply` | `markPendingSync` mutation (on Pub/Sub notification) | Use stored refresh token to call Gmail history.list, fetch new messages |
| `classifyAndRecordReply` | `fetchGmailReply` action | Run keyword classification, create replyDrafts row with status=pending_draft |
| `sendSlackNotification` | After draft is created (status changes to pending_review) | Send Slack interactive message |
| `handleSlackApproval` | Slack callback | Call existing `callUnipileSend`, update draft status |

## RemoteTrigger Specification

- **Schedule:** Every hour (`0 * * * *` UTC)
- **Environment:** Anthropic cloud (env_015asbMBaj4NsdeUxymCXAeR)
- **Model:** claude-sonnet-4-6
- **Prompt:** Fetch pending_draft items from Convex, read voice profile + skills from repo, draft responses, POST drafts back to Convex
- **Tools:** Bash, Read, Glob, Grep (to read repo files for voice/persona context)

## Slack Interactive Message Format

```json
{
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "text": "Reply from Sarah Chen"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "\"Love to learn more about how you handle the migration piece\""
    },
    {
      "type": "divider"
    },
    {
      "type": "section",
      "text": "Draft reply (87 words):\n\nSarah, great to hear..."
    },
    {
      "type": "actions",
      "elements": [
        { "type": "button", "text": "Send", "style": "primary", "action_id": "approve_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Edit", "action_id": "edit_reply" },
        { "type": "button", "text": "Skip", "action_id": "skip_reply" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Security

1. **Gmail refresh tokens** are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. Encryption key is derived from a server-side secret + per-user salt. Never logged or returned in API responses.
2. **Slack bot tokens** are similarly encrypted.
3. **Slack callback verification** uses Slack's signing secret to verify webhook authenticity.
4. **Draft expiry:** Drafts expire after 24 hours to prevent stale responses being sent.

## Edge Cases

1. **Multiple replies in quick succession:** Pub/Sub may batch notifications. `fetchGmailReply` uses `history.list` with the last known historyId, so it catches all messages since the last check, not just the notification trigger.
2. **Token refresh failure:** If the stored refresh token is invalid (user revoked access), mark `gmailCredentials.status = 'expired'` and notify via Slack: "Gmail access expired. Run `nurge connect gmail` to reconnect."
3. **RemoteTrigger down:** If the hourly trigger fails, drafts sit in `pending_draft` state. Next trigger run picks them up. No data loss.
4. **Slack not connected:** If no Slack connection, drafts wait for the next CLI session. The SessionStart hook shows pending drafts.
5. **User edits in Slack:** The Edit button opens a Slack modal with the draft text. User modifies and submits. Convex updates the draft body and sends.
6. **Duplicate sends:** The `callUnipileSend` path already handles deduplication via task status checks. Draft status transitions are atomic (pending_review -> approved -> sent).

## Out of Scope

- Signal monitoring (separate feature, session-only for now)
- Enrichment via RemoteTrigger
- Multi-channel replies (LinkedIn messages)
- Team/multi-user Slack notifications

Base directory for this skill: /Users/rejoesudarsan/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.6/skills/executing-plans

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute all tasks, report when complete.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

**Note:** Tell your human partner that Superpowers works much better with access to subagents. The quality of its work will be significantly higher if run on a platform with subagent support (such as Claude Code or Codex). If subagents are available, use superpowers:subagent-driven-development instead of this skill.

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Tasks

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Stop when blocked, don't guess
- Never start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

    userId: v.string(),
    email: v.string(),

    watchExpiry: v.string(), // ISO8601 -- watch() expires after 7 days

    .index("by_email", ["email"]),

    encryptedRefreshToken: v.string(),
    tokenIv: v.string(),
    scopes: v.string(),
    storedAt: v.string(),
    lastUsedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // active | revoked | expired
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_email", ["email"]),

  // Reply drafts pending user approval (server-side flow)
  replyDrafts: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    replyEventId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    contactName: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.string(),
    originalSubject: v.string(),

    draftBody: v.optional(v.string()),
    draftedBy: v.optional(v.string()), // remote_trigger | template | user
    confidence: v.optional(v.string()), // high | medium | low
    status: v.string(), // pending_draft | pending_review | approved | sent | rejected | expired
    slackMessageTs: v.optional(v.string()),
    notifiedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    reviewedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    sentAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    expiresAt: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_status", ["userId", "status"])
    .index("by_reply_event", ["replyEventId"]),

  // Slack workspace connections for notifications
  slackConnections: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    teamId: v.string(),
    teamName: v.optional(v.string()),
    channelId: v.string(),
    channelName: v.optional(v.string()),
    encryptedBotToken: v.string(),
    botTokenIv: v.string(),
    installedAt: v.string(),
    status: v.string(), // active | revoked
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"]),

  // Deal outcomes from CRM or Gmail (won/lost/stale) for learning
  dealOutcomes: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    source: v.string(),              // hubspot | salesforce | gmail | user
    sourceId: v.optional(v.string()), // CRM deal ID or thread ID
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    company: v.optional(v.string()),
    enrollmentId: v.optional(v.string()),
    outcome: v.string(),             // won | lost | stale
    value: v.optional(v.float64()),
    currency: v.optional(v.string()),
    lossReason: v.optional(v.string()),
    closeDate: v.string(),
    detectedAt: v.string(),
    sourcedBy: v.optional(v.string()),
    sequenceName: v.optional(v.string()),
    touchCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    daysToClose: v.optional(v.float64()),
    personaMatch: v.optional(v.string()),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_contact", ["userId", "contactEmail"])
    .index("by_outcome", ["userId", "outcome"]),
});

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { defineSchema, defineTable } from "convex/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";

export default defineSchema({
  // ── Auth (existing) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  users: defineTable({
    email: v.string(),
    name: v.string(),
    token: v.string(),
    createdAt: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_token", ["token"])
    .index("by_email", ["email"]),

  // ── Execution state (Convex authoritative) ───────────────────────

  emails: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(), // SQLite id
    source: v.string(),
    direction: v.string(),
    threadId: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    fromEmail: v.string(),
    toEmails: v.string(), // JSON array
    ccEmails: v.optional(v.string()),
    date: v.string(),
    snippet: v.optional(v.string()),
    bodyPreview: v.optional(v.string()),
    labels: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Classification (SQLite authoritative, pushed up)
    class: v.optional(v.string()),
    classConfidence: v.optional(v.float64()),
    classifiedBy: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Unipile IDs
    unipileId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileAccountId: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Sync
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"])
    .index("by_thread", ["userId", "threadId"]),

  mailboxes: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    email: v.string(),
    provider: v.string(),
    transportEmail: v.optional(v.string()),
    rotationGroup: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // active | paused | auth_expired
    warmupStage: v.optional(v.string()),
    warmupStartedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    firstTouchDailyCap: v.float64(),
    followUpDailyCap: v.float64(),
    replyDailyCap: v.float64(),
    minGapMinutes: v.float64(),
    sendWindowStartHour: v.float64(),
    sendWindowEndHour: v.float64(),
    sendWindowTimezone: v.string(),
    reputationScore: v.optional(v.float64()),
    priority: v.optional(v.float64()),
    lastUsedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastBounceAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_email", ["userId", "email"]),

  mailboxSendEvents: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.string(), // externalId of mailbox
    sendType: v.string(), // first_touch | follow_up | reply
    sentAt: v.string(),
    taskId: v.optional(v.string()),
    enrollmentId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_mailbox_day", ["mailboxId", "sentAt"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  sequenceEnrollments: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    sequenceName: v.string(),
    campaignName: v.optional(v.string()),
    mailboxId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    contactName: v.optional(v.string()),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // active | paused | completed | stopped
    statusReason: v.optional(v.string()),
    replyState: v.optional(v.string()),
    currentStep: v.float64(),
    totalSteps: v.float64(),
    lastSentAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastReplyAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    nextDueAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    initialBody: v.optional(v.string()),
    // Unipile
    unipileThreadProviderId: v.optional(v.string()),
    unipileAccountId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_status", ["userId", "status"])
    .index("by_contact", ["userId", "contactEmail"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  followUpTasks: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    body: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.optional(v.string()),
    stepNumber: v.float64(),
    stepName: v.optional(v.string()),
    sendType: v.string(),
    status: v.string(), // open | completed | canceled
    sendStatus: v.string(), // pending | pending_remote_send | sent | failed | canceled
    dueAt: v.string(),
    completedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    canceledReason: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastAttemptAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastError: v.optional(v.string()),
    attemptCount: v.float64(),
    // Unipile
    unipileMessageId: v.optional(v.string()),
    providerId: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_due", ["userId", "sendStatus", "dueAt"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  suppressions: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    scope: v.string(), // email | domain
    value: v.string(),
    reason: v.optional(v.string()),
    source: v.string(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_lookup", ["userId", "scope", "value"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  replyEvents: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    mailboxId: v.optional(v.string()),
    threadId: v.string(),
    messageId: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.string(),
    classification: v.string(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    snippet: v.optional(v.string()),
    occurredAt: v.string(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_thread", ["userId", "threadId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  sequenceDrafts: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    enrollmentId: v.string(),
    taskId: v.string(),
    stepNumber: v.float64(),
    subject: v.optional(v.string()),
    body: v.optional(v.string()),
    channel: v.string(), // email | linkedin
    status: v.string(), // pending | sent | canceled | stale
    generatedBy: v.optional(v.string()),
    generatedContext: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_task", ["taskId"])
    .index("by_enrollment", ["enrollmentId"])
    .index("by_sync_version", ["syncVersion"]),

  // ── Local state (SQLite authoritative, pushed up for dashboard) ──

  contacts: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    source: v.string(),
    sourceId: v.optional(v.string()),
    email: v.string(),
    name: v.optional(v.string()),
    domain: v.optional(v.string()),
    company: v.optional(v.string()),
    title: v.optional(v.string()),
    phone: v.optional(v.string()),
    leadStatus: v.optional(v.string()),
    owner: v.optional(v.string()),
    lastActivityAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    emailCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    lastEmailedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
    class: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_email", ["userId", "email"]),

  learnings: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    type: v.string(),
    content: v.string(),
    sourceSession: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  skillVersions: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    skillName: v.string(),
    version: v.float64(),
    content: v.string(),
    emailCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    learningCount: v.optional(v.float64()),
    active: v.optional(v.float64()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  deals: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),
    source: v.string(),
    sourceId: v.string(),
    name: v.string(),
    contactEmail: v.optional(v.string()),
    company: v.optional(v.string()),
    stage: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.optional(v.string()),
    value: v.optional(v.float64()),
    currency: v.optional(v.string()),
    closeDate: v.optional(v.string()),
    owner: v.optional(v.string()),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),

  // ── Context metrics (SQLite authoritative, daily rollups pushed) ──

  contextMetricsDaily: defineTable({
    externalId: v.string(),     // "2026-03-25:emails list"
    day: v.string(),            // "2026-03-25"
    command: v.string(),        // "emails list"
    calls: v.float64(),
    totalBytes: v.float64(),
    totalTokens: v.float64(),
    syncVersion: v.float64(),
    userId: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
    .index("by_day", ["userId", "day"])
    .index("by_command", ["userId", "command"]),

  // ── Convex-only tables ───────────────────────────────────────────

  unipileAccounts: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    provider: v.string(), // GOOGLE | LINKEDIN
    unipileAccountId: v.string(),
    email: v.optional(v.string()),
    status: v.string(), // connected | disconnected | auth_expired
    connectedAt: v.string(),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_provider", ["userId", "provider"])
    .index("by_unipile_id", ["unipileAccountId"]),

  syncState: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    lastSyncVersion: v.float64(),
  }).index("by_user", ["userId"]),

  // Gmail Pub/Sub push notification state
  gmailWatchState: defineTable({
    userId: v.string(),
    email: v.string(),
    historyId: v.string(),
    watchExpiry: v.string(), // ISO8601 -- watch() expires after 7 days
    pendingSync: v.boolean(), // true when Pub/Sub notification received but not yet synced
    lastNotifiedAt: v.optional(v.string()),
  })
    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
    .index("by_email", ["email"]),
});

feat: add gmailCredentials, replyDrafts, slackConnections, dealOutcomes tables

New Convex tables for server-side reply handling:
- gmailCredentials: encrypted Gmail refresh tokens for server-side API access
- replyDrafts: draft responses pending user approval via Slack
- slackConnections: Slack workspace connections for notifications
- dealOutcomes: won/lost/stale outcomes from CRM or Gmail for learning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
1→package connector
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"crypto/aes"
     5→	"crypto/cipher"
     6→	"crypto/rand"
     7→	"encoding/hex"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"os"
    10→
    11→	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/account"
    14→)
    15→
    16→// PushGmailTokenToConvex encrypts the refresh token and sends it to Convex
    17→// for server-side Gmail API access (reply detection without local machine).
    18→func PushGmailTokenToConvex(token *oauth2.Token, email string) error {
    19→	if token.RefreshToken == "" {
    20→		return nil // No refresh token, skip silently
    21→	}
    22→
    23→	encKey := os.Getenv("CONVEX_ENCRYPTION_KEY")
    24→	if encKey == "" {
    25→		// Server-side features not configured, skip silently
    26→		return nil
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	encrypted, iv, err := encryptAESGCM(token.RefreshToken, encKey)
    30→	if err != nil {
    31→		return fmt.Errorf("encrypting token: %w", err)
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	client := account.NewConvexClient()
    35→	return client.Post("/api/store-gmail-credentials", map[string]string{
    36→		"email":                 email,
    37→		"encryptedRefreshToken": encrypted,
    38→		"tokenIv":              iv,
    39→		"scopes":               "gmail.readonly,gmail.send",
    40→	})
    41→}
    42→
    43→func encryptAESGCM(plaintext, keyHex string) (string, string, error) {
    44→	key, err := hex.DecodeString(keyHex)
    45→	if err != nil {
    46→		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid key hex: %w", err)
    47→	}
    48→	if len(key) != 32 {
    49→		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("key must be 32 bytes (64 hex chars), got %d", len(key))
    50→	}
    51→
    52→	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    53→	if err != nil {
    54→		return "", "", err
    55→	}
    56→
    57→	aesGCM, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    58→	if err != nil {
    59→		return "", "", err
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	iv := make([]byte, aesGCM.NonceSize())
    63→	if _, err := rand.Read(iv); err != nil {
    64→		return "", "", err
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	ciphertext := aesGCM.Seal(nil, iv, []byte(plaintext), nil)
    68→
    69→	return hex.EncodeToString(ciphertext), hex.EncodeToString(iv), nil
    70→}
    71→
